page: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 Intel Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The instance is equipped with 5.0 GB of system memory and 90 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Acceleration is provided by a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, delivering 2 GB of dedicated VRAM.

Performance benchmarks indicate a highly specialized profile. While general CPU and memory operations score in the lower percentiles—such as a Passmark CPU score of 1849.31 and memory read bandwidth of 13.71 GB/sec for large blocks—the GPU acceleration enables exceptional performance in specific workloads. LLM inference benchmarks for a small 135M model reach top-tier speeds of 9931.92 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 454.57 tokens/sec for text generation. Prompt processing for a medium 7B model also registers a strong 211.23 tokens/sec.

This instance offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated computing by utilizing a fractional GPU allocation and a shared CPU core, avoiding the expense of a full dedicated GPU. However, the tradeoff is limited CPU compute power and memory bandwidth, making it unsuitable for heavy multi-threaded CPU tasks or large-scale database operations. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, small-scale machine learning model testing, and development workloads requiring basic GPU acceleration.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It features a single shared Intel Broadwell vCPU clocked at 2.0 GHz, 5.0 GB of RAM, and 90 GB of NVMe SSD storage. Graphics and compute acceleration are handled by a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB of VRAM. While general CPU and memory benchmarks are modest, the instance excels in small-scale LLM inference, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds of 9931.92 tokens/sec on a 135M model. This configuration offers a cost-efficient option for developers needing GPU access without the expense of dedicated hardware, making it ideal for lightweight model testing, development, and basic inference tasks.

1 shared Intel Broadwell vCPU and 5.0 GB of system memory
90 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast data access
